description_long: This object is an old, weathered gas pump with a rectangular body and a flat, overhanging top canopy. The front face features faded labels, buttons, and a display panel, with a single fuel hose and nozzle hanging from the side. The pump is mounted on a thick, worn base that appears to be made of concrete or stone, showing signs of grime and age. The top canopy is painted red with white, partially legible text, and the metal surfaces are rusted and stained, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The back and sides are mostly plain, with some rust and a small access panel visible. The overall appearance suggests a vintage, self-service gas pump commonly found at older or abandoned urban gas stations.
description: A vintage, weathered gas pump with a red canopy, faded labels, and a single fuel hose, mounted on a thick, worn base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main display panel, faded labels, buttons, and the fuel hose with nozzle hanging to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view revealing a rusted metal surface, a small access panel, and the hose draped along the side.
description_view_2: Side view displaying a mostly plain, stained surface with no visible controls or hose.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a rusted surface, a small access panel, and the fuel hose lying on the ground.
attribute_fuel_pumps: One visible vintage-style fuel pump with a rectangular body, red canopy, single hose and nozzle, faded labels, and analog-style buttons and display. No digital screens or modern branding are visible.
attribute_station_signage: Faded white text on the red canopy, partially legible as 'Self-Serve' and other indistinct letters. No other signage or branding is visible.
